sql_prompt_rules: Use only metadata-backed Oracle tables, columns, and join paths.
Do not force mandatory filter obligations; generate SQL aligned strictly to the user request.
Return executable SQL statements with FETCH FIRST 200 ROWS ONLY and no semicolon.
time_expression_guidance: If request contains YYYY (year), use a year predicate with :year_value and YYYY mask logic.
If request contains YYYYMM or asks for month scope, use a month predicate with :report_period and YYYYMM mask logic.
If request contains YYYYMMDD or day scope, use a day predicate with :date_value and YYYYMMDD mask logic.
Select metadata-valid date columns for these predicates; do not invent columns.

Date columns in production tables are surrogate keys (*_TARIH_ID). Always JOIN to AS_DWH.GNL_TARIH on TARIH_ID to access date values. Never fabricate direct date columns like EK_TANZIM_TARIHI or TANZIM_TARIHI. Pattern: JOIN AS_DWH.GNL_TARIH t ON <table>.TANZIM_TARIH_ID = t.TARIH_ID WHERE TO_CHAR(t.TARIH, 'YYYYMM') = :report_period
